plan: Trim SPY to 20% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Increase QQQI to 25% for more income and stability.
Add to QQQ, targeting 20%, to enhance broad index exposure.
Trim CRWD to 10% to reduce single name concentration.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~15-20% for capital preservation.
conservative.read: This conservative plan aims for capital preservation, targeting ~1.5%/month with significantly reduced drawdown risk. It eliminates all leveraged ETFs and focuses on broad index and income for stability. Expect lower volatility, but also more modest growth potential.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet concentration limits and reduce overall exposure.
Sell SOXL and SPAL to remove underperforming leveraged ETFs.
Increase QQQI to 22% for a balanced income component.
Add to CRWD, targeting 15%, to lean into a strong AI/Software name.
Initiate a new position in TSLA at 5% based on its strong scanner read.
Maintain a cash buffer of ~10% for flexibility and risk management.
balanced.read: This balanced plan aims for risk-first growth, targeting ~2.5%/month with moderate drawdown potential. It reduces leverage and concentration while blending broad index with quality tech. Expect a balance of growth and stability, with controlled risk.

moves.note: Monitor high R/R setup for entry

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to meet the concentration mandate.
Add to SOXL, targeting 7%, to increase leveraged semiconductor exposure.
Increase SMH to 18% to further lean into the semiconductor sector.
Add to CRWD, targeting 18%, to press a high-scoring AI/Software name.
Initiate a new position in TSLA at 10% based on its strong scanner read.
Watch NVDU for a potential 5% buy, given its high risk/reward setup.
aggressive.read: This aggressive plan targets stretch growth, aiming for ~4%/month, but comes with significantly higher drawdown risk. It uses and adds leveraged ETFs and concentrates in high-scoring setups. Be aware that leverage amplifies both gains and losses, and leveraged ETFs experience daily decay, requiring strict stop discipline.

plan: Trim SPY to 25% to reduce concentration and comply with the mandate.
Sell all SOXL and SPAL to eliminate leveraged ETF exposure.
Sell CRWD to reduce high-beta single name exposure.
Trim SMH to 5% to significantly reduce high-beta sector exposure.
Increase QQQI to 40% to maximize income generation.
Maintain a cash buffer for stability and monthly distributions.
income.read: This income plan aims to park cash for yield, targeting ~1%/month with a focus on steady monthly distributions. It significantly reduces leverage and high-beta exposure, prioritizing low drawdown. Remember, distributions are variable and NAV can still fall in a selloff; this is income, not a savings account.
